hook.text: Excision vs. Ablation Here's what the endo nurse wants you to know
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Appeals to authority of a nurse specialist and creates curiosity about insider knowledge
hook.target_audience_raw: People with endometriosis or considering surgery, evaluating treatment options

main_claim.text: Excision is necessary because endometriosis is too deep to be treated effectively by ablation, which only removes the surface layer.

supporting_explanation.summary: Ablation is compared to skimming a wall with blown plaster — it doesn't address the underlying damage. Endometriosis tissue is deep-rooted, so superficial ablation leads to symptom recurrence.

funnel_rationale: The video explains a specific treatment comparison (excision vs ablation) aimed at patients who are already diagnosed or evaluating surgery, not broad awareness.
